JavaScript tests for `Math.acos` function with various inputs to verify NaN and +0.0 return values.


## Files
- **[15.08.02.02-001.js](15.08.02.02-001.js.md)**: Tests that `Math.acos(NaN)` returns `NaN` using an assertion.
- **[15.08.02.02-002.js](15.08.02.02-002.js.md)**: Tests the `Math.acos` function with a value slightly greater than 1 to ensure it returns NaN.
- **[15.08.02.02-003.js](15.08.02.02-003.js.md)**: Tests that Math.acos returns NaN for Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY.
- **[15.08.02.02-004.js](15.08.02.02-004.js.md)**: Tests the Math.acos function to verify it returns +0.0 when the input is 1.
- **[15.08.02.02-005.js](15.08.02.02-005.js.md)**: Tests the Math.acos function to confirm it returns NaN for inputs slightly greater than 1.
- **[15.08.02.02-006.js](15.08.02.02-006.js.md)**: Tests that `Math.acos(-7)` returns `NaN` in JavaScript.
